title: Echo
content:
"Who called?" I said, and the words
    Through the whispering glades,
 Hither, thither, baffled the birds - 
    "Who called? Who called?"

 The leafy boughs on high
    Hissed in the sun;
 The dark air carried my cry
    Faintingly on:

 Eyes in the green, in the shade,
    In the motionless brake,
 Voices that said what I said,
    For mockery's sake:

 "Who cares?" I bawled through my tears;
    The wind fell low:
 In the silence, "Who cares? who cares?"
    Wailed to and fro.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
